docs.title: Welcome to B-Gyro
docs.text: "Meet B-Gyro: our attempt at an operating system inspired by the gyroscope's stability... except ours wobbles more than it balances. Think of it as a gyroscope that moonlights as a boomerang—it keeps coming back with more bugs to fix!" -- by 0rayn and faithByte

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• 32-bit operating system
  • Developed by 0rayn and faithByte
  • Debugging system using serial communication.
  • partial-support for Ansi escape codes, for colored text.
  • Minimalistic shell, with basic commands to interact with the system.

docs.title: About Us
docs.text: We are a team of passionate developers working on creating a unique operating system. Our goal is to learn, innovate, and have fun along the way.

docs.title: Get Started
docs.text: To get started with B-Gyro, check out our documentation.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: For any inquiries or feedback, feel free to reach out to us at b.gyro.os@gmail.com

docs.title: Welcome to B-Gyro
docs.text: this document is a guide to help you get started with B-Gyro. If you have any questions or need help, feel free to reach out to us at b.gyro.os@gmail.com

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Before you begin, ensure you have met the following requirements: - docker and docker-compose installed on your machine. - qemu-system-x86_64 installed on your machine.

docs.title: play-test:
docs.text:
To play-test B-Gyro, follow these steps:

  1. just clone the repository and run the following command: 
    make
  2. To run the operating system, run the following command: 
    make run
